title: BEM-876 auditor review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM876_AUDITOR_PROTOCOL_V14_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-876 AUDITOR PROTOCOL V1.4 REVIEW
Status: AUDITOR_REVIEW_READY
Findings:
- Operator objection is valid: v1.4 must not replace v1.2 with details; it must preserve v1.2 as the upper-level frame and add v1.3/v1.4 details below it.
- Куратор must not be defined only as Worker node. Correct definition: Curator is a management component inside every role-object: General Director, Director, Worker.
- Use one terminology layer: role-object, internal component, internal contour, workspace, task registry, event log, rulebook.
- Replace управляющая плоскость with управляющий контур throughout the main text.
- Replace реестр времени with журнал событий.
- GitHub Issues must not be the canonical task registry due scale limits; mention only as temporary implementation example, not architecture.
- Free contour-to-contour exchange must be excluded from main decision text; selected model is managed shared channel under Curator policy.
- Rulebook must include error condition, detection markers, approved action, forbidden action, owner, version, source incident, and conflict policy.
- General Director writes rules for Directors; Director writes rules for Workers; Worker Curator applies rules to internal contours and reports incidents upward.
- Roadmap must include concrete IDs for building General Director, Director, Worker, Curator component, task registry, rulebook, event log, architecture registry, workspaces, testing contour, product repo link.
Risks:
- Too many new concepts can reduce readability. Use tables and definitions.
- If rule conditions are too rigid, elements may misapply rules. Use conditions as matching hints plus confidence and escalation rule.
Recommendation:
- Approve v1.4 with notes: keep Russian-first terminology, clickable TOC, version history, and section-by-section traceability from operator remarks to protocol changes.
No issue comments.
